# gss_import_sec_context(_:_:_:)

**Function**

Imports a security context from another process.

## Declaration

```swift
func gss_import_sec_context(_ minor_status: UnsafeMutablePointer<OM_uint32>, _ interprocess_token: gss_buffer_t, _ context_handle: UnsafeMutablePointer<gss_ctx_id_t?>) -> OM_uint32
```

## Parameters

- **minor_status**: A pointer to the secondary status result that provides additional information in case of failure.
- **interprocess_token**: The token created by a call to [doc://com.apple.gss/documentation/GSS/gss_export_sec_context(_:_:_:)].
- **context_handle**: A pointer the function uses to return the imported context. Release the context with the [doc://com.apple.gss/documentation/GSS/gss_delete_sec_context(_:_:_:)] function when you are done with it.

## Return Value

A status code set to [doc://com.apple.gss/documentation/GSS/GSS_S_COMPLETE] on success. See [doc://com.apple.gss/documentation/GSS/function-status] for a description of other status outputs.

## Discussion

You can import an interprocess token only once. After you have imported it, release the token’s buffer using a call to [doc://com.apple.gss/documentation/GSS/gss_release_buffer(_:_:)].

## Import and Export

- **gss_export_sec_context(_:_:_:)**: Transfers a security context to another process.
